# AI Wrapped

> Privacy-first monthly wrap of your AI usage. Upload chat exports from ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, Gemini, Claude Code, and Cursor to see estimated time spent and time saved. Parsing runs entirely in the browser — files are never sent to our servers.

## What it does

AI Wrapped parses AI chat export files locally in the user's browser and generates a monthly summary showing:
- Estimated time spent interacting with AI tools
- Estimated time saved compared to doing the work manually
- Category breakdowns (coding, writing, research, etc.)
- Top tasks within each category

## Supported sources

- ChatGPT (ZIP export from Settings → Data Controls)
- Claude (ZIP export from Settings → Privacy)
- Grok (ZIP export from accounts.x.ai/data)
- Gemini (JSON via Google Takeout → My Activity → Gemini Apps)
- Claude Code (local .jsonl session files from ~/.claude/)
- Cursor (local .db / .vscdb files)

## Key properties

- Free to use, no signup required
- Chats and exports are never sent to our servers — there are no servers; parsing and analysis are 100% client-side
- No files are uploaded — everything is processed locally in the browser
- No account; anonymous page-view analytics (Google Tag Manager) only — uploaded data is never sent to our servers
- Optional LLM classification is opt-in: conversation snippets go directly from the browser to OpenAI using the user's own API key

## Pages

- [Home](https://theaiwrapped.com/): Upload exports and generate a wrap
- [History](https://theaiwrapped.com/history): View past wraps stored locally
- [Methodology](https://theaiwrapped.com/methodology): How time-saved estimates are calculated
- [Settings](https://theaiwrapped.com/settings): Configure preferences
